UNI CRISIS: Finding a desk at Kate Edgar Level 3

The search for a study space

Kate Edgar Level 3 is a popular study area, but finding a desk can sometimes feel harder than expected. I collected observational data to explore what the space looked like at different times, focusing on unattended belongings, available seats, student activity, and the general study environment.

POV Walking up and down the rows

Are desks being claimed by belongings?

This visualisation shows how the number of unattended bags or personal items changed across different times of day. It helps show whether desk-saving behaviour was more visible during certain periods.

A Bar Chart showing the number of unattended bags or personal items throughout the day

Are there actually seats available?

This plot compares visible empty study seats with unattended belongings. This helps show whether the space only looked busy, or whether there were still usable seats available.

A Scatter Plot comparing the number of unoccupied seats with the number unattended personal items/bags

What are students mostly doing?

This visualisation gives context to the study environment by showing what students were mostly doing during each observation. This helps connect the physical space with the behaviour happening around it.

A Clustered Column graph which illustrates the current study environment
